writeup: June 8, 2026 marked the eve of the Buffalo Bills' mandatory minicamp — the most consequential stretch of their offseason under first-year head coach Joe Brady. With the three-day camp set to open the very next morning, the buzz in Western New York centered on whether new defensive coordinator Jim Leonhard's revamped scheme would take shape, and how key injured players like Ed Oliver would fare. ESPN's league-wide minicamp preview spotlighted Buffalo as one of 17 teams kicking off mandatory workouts that week, underscoring the franchise's high-profile transition away from the Sean McDermott era. On the eve of camp, storylines were already swirling around roster battles, a new-look receiving corps headlined by DJ Moore, and the identity of a defense undergoing a full philosophical overhaul.

articles.title: 2026 NFL minicamps: Teams, rookies, QBs and more we're watching
